channel.title: Emanuele De Cupis
channel.description: but you may know me as Balanza

item.title: Reuse code in Alloy using Grunt and Git-submodules
item.description:
Code reutilization is a key aspect of every project.

Code reutilization is the business write once (DRY) tested code

In this article we’ll cover different methods and tips for implementing best practices in code reutilization in Alloy, as it has been released. Then, we’ll walk throught a tecnique I often use in my projects, having very good

Code reutilization in Alloy

Alloy itself is meant to help developers implement best practices in project source management. By building an MVC layer over a classical Titanium project and with its extensive use of CommonJS’ module pattern, it drives developer to code reutiliza.
Built-in solution to reuse code and keep development DRY are: - CommonJS modules - Widgets CommonJS is the standard de facto for dependency injection in Java script. It encapsulates functionalities, keeping code tidy and preventing global scope flooding (*) issue. More over, package managers like npm make managing references extremely easy, thus you can both reduce coupling and increase cohesion in your source. Have you can learn how to create and reference CommonJS modules in Alloy. It’s also plenty of third party modules on gittio Widgets are meant to be reusable UI components. They’re basically an atomic Alloy MVC entity - a view, a controller, a stylesheet, optionally a model. Although they can grow in complexity by including other modules, controllers, views, libraries and Widgets, They’re a limited to just one use (cioè sono un componente solo)

Using submodules: issues: want to reuse controller/view/model

Custom: grunt the problem intoducing grunt custom merge drive throught: repo_a, repo_b

Conclusion
